todaysEvents.summary: Iran launched 15 ballistic missiles toward Saudi Aramco's Dhahran and Abqaiq facilities on Mar 22; Saudi Patriot batteries intercepted 12; 3 struck perimeter with no production impact confirmed by Aramco (Al Jazeera, Reuters). (Mar 22)

Arab League emergency summit convened in Cairo on Mar 22; Egypt and Jordan calling for ceasefire; Iran FM rejected negotiations 'under military threat'; Saudi Arabia maintaining 'right to defend' but holding from further strikes pending a diplomatic track (Reuters). (Mar 22)
todaysEvents.delta: Arab League emergency summit in Cairo

todaysEvents.summary: China issued rare public statement urging 'maximum restraint from all parties' and offered to mediate; US rejected China mediation; EU called for unconditional ceasefire; Japan announced emergency release from national oil stockpile (Reuters, Kyodo News). (Mar 22)
todaysEvents.delta: China mediation offer; US rejects; Japan SPR

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Saudi Arabia and Iran both pull back following the Abqaiq missile intercept; Arab League ceasefire initiative finds traction. Bilateral Hormuz transit deals revive in degraded form; Qatar LNG hub damage contained. Brent retreats toward $105-115/bbl on ceasefire signal. JKM retreats from $23.40 as Australian LNG ramps. P&I cover restoration begins within 30 days. ASEAN oil-importer FX stabilises. US and Saudi diplomatic channel produces temporary standstill agreement.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Saudi limited strikes on IRGC facilities continue; Iran sustains low-intensity missile harassment of Saudi oil infrastructure. Ras Laffan restart delayed 6-10 weeks. Brent holds $125-145/bbl; JKM at $23-27/MMBtu with no Qatar restart path. ASEAN oil-importer FX (IDR, PHP, THB) under sustained pressure; iTraxx Asia IG tests 200bp. Arab League ceasefire resolution produced but not implemented. IEA second emergency release provides only marginal price relief.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Iran lands a damaging strike on Abqaiq or Ras Tanura, triggering Saudi full military entry; IRGC activates Strait of Malacca closure threat. Full commercial Hormuz and Red Sea blockade; Ras Laffan permanently offline for the quarter. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges — Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and sovereign credit-rating action. China forced to choose sides; US-Iran direct naval engagement in Gulf of Oman. P&I cover unavailable through H2 2026.
